hypothesis: Training template-free retrosynthesis models with a class-balanced loss that weights each reaction by the inverse effective number of its local template occurrences will increase top-10 accuracy on rare templates (frequency ≤10) by at least 15 percentage points and at least double OOD top-10 accuracy on unseen templates, without reducing top-10 accuracy on frequent templates (frequency ≥10,001) by more than 2 percentage points.
